briefdescription.para: This macro selects the bridge YUV420 mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem
is a flag to enable or disable YUV420 mode

briefdescription.para: This macro selects the bridge pixel repeat mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem
is a flag to enable or disable pixel drop mode

briefdescription.para: Define Driver instance of all sub-core included in the design.
This function queries the subsystem instance configuration to determine the included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: For each sub-core that is present in the design the sub-core driver instance is binded with the subsystem sub-core driver handle

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is the delay/sleep function for the XV_HdmiRxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: For the Zynq family, there exists native sleep functionality. For MicroBlaze however, there does not exist such functionality. In the MicroBlaze case, the default method for delaying is to use a predetermined amount of loop iterations. This method is prone to inaccuracy and dependent on system configuration; for greater accuracy, the user may supply their own delay/sleep handler, pointed to by InstancePtr->UserTimerWaitUs, which may have better accuracy if a hardware timer is used.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HdmiSsRx instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the Vendor Specific Info Frame.
This function register's all sub-core ISR's with interrupt controller and any subsystem level call back function with requisite sub-core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a RX connect event has occurred.
This function is called when a Bridge overflow event has occurred.
This function is called when a RX AUX IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when a RX Audio IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when a RX Link Status IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when a RX DDC IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when the RX stream is down.
This function is called when the RX stream init .
This function is called when the RX stream is up.
This function is called when a RX Sync Loss IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when the mode has transitioned from DVI to HDMI or vice versa.
This function is called when the TMDS CLK ratio changes.
This function is called when the Vic does not match with the detected video timing.
This function is called when a PHY error has occurred (e.g.

detaileddescription.para: stream up timeout while locked; application may reset the video PHY).

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of cores included in Video Processing Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the HDMI RX SS timing information.
This function reports the link quality based on the link error counter.
This function prints the HDMI RX SS DRM If information.
This function prints the HDMI RX SS audio information.
This function prints the HDMI RX SS audio information.
This function prints the HDMI RX SS subcore versions.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the Bridge for YUV420 functionality and repeater.

parameterdescription.para: Instance Pointer to the main data structure

briefdescription.para: This function installs a custom delay/sleep function to be used by the XV_HdmiRxSs driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HdmiSsRx instance.
is the address to the callback function.
is the user data item (microseconds to delay) that will be passed to the custom sleep/delay function when it is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function calls the interrupt handler for HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the video subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization is successful else XST_FAILURE

briefdescription.para: This function starts the HDMI RX subsystem including all sub-cores that are included in the processing pipeline for a given use-case.

detaileddescription.para:
Video pipe is started from back to front
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
None
Cores are started only if the corresponding start flag in the scratch pad memory is set. This allows to selectively start only those cores included in the processing chain

briefdescription.para: This function stops the HDMI RX subsystem including all sub-cores Stop the video pipe starting from front to back.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the video subsystem sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para:
There are 2 reset networks within the subsystem
For cores that are on AXIS interface
For cores that are on AXI-MM interface

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the Internal Video reset of the HDMI subcore within the subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is a flag to assert (TRUE) or release (FALSE) the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the Internal Link reset of the HDMI subcore within the subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is a flag to assert (TRUE) or release (FALSE) the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the video reset of other blocks within the subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is a flag to assert (TRUE) or release (FALSE) the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the system reset of other blocks within the subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is a flag to assert (TRUE) or release (FALSE) the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function Sets the EDID parameters in the HDMI RX SS struct.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.
is a pointer to the EDID data buffer.
is the length of the EDID data buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function loads the default EDID to the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function loads the specified EDID to the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.
is a pointer to the EDID data buffer to be loaded.
is the length of the EDID data buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HPD on the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

is a flag used to set the HPD.
TRUE drives HPD high
FALSE drives HPD low

briefdescription.para: This function toggles the HPD on the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS Aux structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function writes to SCDC registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.
is the SCDC register address to write to.
is the data value to write to the register.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS AVI InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS General Control Packet structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS Audio InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS Vendor Specific InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS DRM InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function set HDMI RX subsystem stream parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.
is the TMDS clock value.
is the line rate value.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if stream parameters set successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video Identification code.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream type.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream type.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDMI RX SS number of active audio channels.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDMI RX SS audio format.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function is called when HDMI RX SS TMDS clock changes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the video stream is up.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the interface is connected.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.

listitem.para: FALSE if interface is not connected.

briefdescription.para: This function will set the default in HDF.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs core instance.
is the XV_HdmiRxSs ID to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function will set PPC specified by user.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs core instance.
is the XV_HdmiRxSs ID to operate on.
is the PPC to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function will set the major and minor application version in RXSs struct.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs core instance.
is the major version of the application.
is the minor version of the application.

briefdescription.para: This function set HDMI RX audio parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs instance.
0: Unmute the audio 1: Mute the audio.

briefdescription.para: This is main code of Xilinx HDMI Receiver Subsystem device driver.

detaileddescription.para: Please see xv_hdmirxss.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 10/07/15 Initial release. 1.1 yh 15/01/16 Added 3D Video support 1.2 yh 20/01/16 Added remapper support 1.3 yh 01/02/16 Added set_ppc api 1.4 yh 01/02/16 Removed xil_print "Cable (dis)connected" 1.5 yh 01/02/16 Removed xil_printf("Active audio channels...) 1.6 yh 15/02/16 Added default value to XV_HdmiRxSs_ConfigRemapper 1.7 MG 03/02/16 Added HDCP support 1.8 MG 10/02/16 Moved HDCP 2.2 reset from stream up/down callback to connect callback 1.9 MH 15/03/16 Added HDCP authenticated callback support 1.10 MH 23/04/16 1. HDCP 1.x driver now uses AXI timer 4.1, so updated to use AXI Timer config structure to determine timer clock frequency 2. HDCP 1.x driver has fixed the problem where the reset for the receiver causes the entire DDC peripheral to get reset. Based on this change the driver has been updated to use XV_HdmiRxSs_HdcpReset and XV_HdmiRxSs_HdcpReset functions directly. 3. Updated XV_HdmiRxSs_HdcpEnable and XV_HdmiRxSs_HdcpEnable functions to ensure that HDCP 1.4 and 2.2 are mutually exclusive. This fixes the problem where HDCP 1.4 and 2.2 state machines are running simultaneously. 1.11 MG 13/05/16 Added DDC peripheral HDCP mode selection to XV_HdmiRxSs_HdcpEnable 1.12 MH 23/06/16 Added HDCP repeater support. 1.13 YH 18/07/16 1. Replace xil_print with xdbg_printf. 2. Replace MB_Sleep() with usleep() 1.14 YH 25/07/16 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for BaseAddress XV_HdmiRxSs_CfgInitialize 1.15 MH 26/07/16 Updates for automatic protocol switching 1.16 MH 05/08/16 Updates to optimize out HDCP when excluded 1.17 YH 17/08/16 Remove sleep in XV_HdmiRxSs_ResetRemapper squash unused variable compiler warning Added Event Log 1.18 MH 08/10/16 Improve HDCP 1.4 authentication 1.19 MG 31/10/16 Fixed issue with reference clock compensation in XV_HdmiRxSS_SetStream 1.20 YH 14/11/16 Added API to enable/disable YUV420/Pixel Drop Mode for video bridge 1.21 YH 14/11/16 Remove Remapper APIs Replace XV_HdmiRxSs_ConfigRemapper API with XV_HdmiRxSs_ConfigBridgeMode API as remapper feature is moved to video bridge and controlled by HDMI core 1.22 MMO 03/01/17 Add compiler option(XV_HDMIRXSS_LOG_ENABLE) to enable Log Move global variable XV_HdmiRx_VSIF VSIF to local XV_HdmiRxSs_RetrieveVSInfoframe API Move HDCP related API's to hdmirxss_hdcp.c 1.23 MMO 10/02/17 Added Sync Loss and HDMI/DVI Interrupt Support
1.4 YH 07/07/17 Add new log type XV_HDMIRXSS_LOG_EVT_SETSTREAM_ERR Report HDMI/DVI mode in HDMI example design info log

1.41 MMO 21/07/17 CR-979900 (Fix) Removed the HDCP Push Event API Call when the Aux Callback event happen MH 09/08/17 Added function XV_HdmiRxSs_HdcpSetCapability 1.42 YH 06/10/17 Added function XV_HdmiRxSs_GetAudioFormat EB 10/10/17 Updated function XV_HdmiRxSs_ReportAudio to report audio format 5.00 YH 16/11/17 Added dedicated reset for each clock domain 16/11/17 Added bridge overflow interrupt EB 16/01/18 Added parsing of InfoFrames during AuxCallback Changed XV_HdmiRxSs_RetrieveVSInfoframe's input parameter type Added function XV_HdmiRxSs_GetAviInfoframe, XV_HdmiRxSs_GetGCP, XV_HdmiRxSs_GetAudioInfoframe, XV_HdmiRxSs_GetVSIF Updated XV_HdmiRxSs_ConfigBridgeMode so Pixel Pepetition is based on received AVI InfoFrame SM 28/02/18 Added definition of XV_HdmiRxSS_SetAppVersion() API 5.10 MMO 06/04/18 Updated XV_HdmiRxSs_ToggleHpd and XV_HdmiRxSs_Stop for cleaner HPD flow during transition from HDMI2.0 to HDMI1.4 YH 13/04/18 Fixed a bug in XV_HdmiRxSs_BrdgOverflowCallback 5.20 EB 03/08/18 Added function XV_HdmiRxSs_AudioMute Added TMDS Clock Ratio callback support 5.40 EB 06/08/19 Added Vic and Video Timing mismatch callback support
